results.title: Mum How Do You Spell Gorbatrof? (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Pamela Williams, Ron Saunders and Film Australia
results.brief_description: A wonderful Story that looks at the way one young Australian boy, Eamen Burke, pushes his capacity to speak up for himself in a world careering towards destruction. Eamen’s message is simple: “If people have the capacity to make bombs, then they should have the capacity to take them apart again - there’s so much beauty in the world that it would be a shame to spoil it”.

results.title: Mum's xmas
results.creator_credit: William Walker and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: Christmas, 1970, and things look grim for Peter, Robert, Rita and Dad. As their expectant mother spells it out, a terminally ill grandparent, unemployed father, big eared grumpy guest and a present for two that has to be shared between three. Things are bound to go wrong, as they are astutely observed by eldest son, Robert. Cast includes Tina Owens, Michael and Jason Condon, Rodney Sharp and Tara Dixon.

results.title: Multiply and subdue the earth
results.creator_credit: Austin Hoyt, Ron Blau, Public Broadcasting Lab and WGBH Educational Foundation
results.brief_description: Man must use ecological planning and seek unity with nature, not conquest of it. Our cities are overcrowded, much of the air we breathe is polluted, and the source of our future supply of food and water is in danger. These problems are caused, in part, by unplanned use of our natural environment.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Austin Hoyt, Ron Blau, Public Broadcasting Lab and WGBH Educational Foundation

results.title: Multiple man
results.creator_credit: Claude Godbout, Georges Dufaux, Clement Perron, Robert Forget and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: A many-faced view of humanity, of global man in all his forms and interests. Produced originally in 70 mm (with stereophonic sound) for showing at Man and His World, the Montreal fair that succeeded Expo 67, this film employs the multi-image technique. People of all places, origins, cultures, secular and religious, are here united and seen side by side, creating an impressive, inspiring and challenging portrait. The film’s title appears in seven languages. Film without words. (Awards: six including Barcelona; Burenos Aires; Philadelphia)

results.title: Multiple intelligences and Australian classrooms
results.creator_credit: Chris Perry, Helen McGrath and Course Development Centre, Deakin University
results.brief_description: Intelligence tests have traditionally concentrated in the narrow fields of language, mathematics and logic skill. This video provides snapshots of how teachers are using psychologist Howard Gardner’s model to improve their taching and recognise and assess particular strengths of each of their pupils. Gardner’s model suggests there are seven intelligences: Linguistic, Logical-mathematical, Visual-spatial, Musical, Bodily-Kinaesthetic, Interpersonal and Intrapersonal.

results.title: Multimedia Stravinsky: the rite of spring (PC)
results.brief_description: This is an insightful multimedia exploration of Stravinsky’s passionate composition. Explore the ballet, the music and the cultural milieu that gave birth to Stravinky’s art. SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS: A multimedia PC with 386SX or higher miscroprocessor, 2MB of RAM, 30-MB hard disk, CD-ROM drive, audio board, VGA display, Microsoft Windows operating system (3.1 or later), MS-DOS operating system version 3.1 or later.

results.title: Multimedia Strauss: three tone poems (PC)
results.brief_description: Welcome to Multimedia Strauss. You will discover the beauty of three of Richard Strauss’ most popular - and powerful - tone poems. SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS: To hear the music and narration and to display the graphics and text you need: a multimedia PC that includes a 386SX or higher processor, 4MB of RAM, 5MB free hard disk space, CD-ROM drive, audio board capable of playing ADPCM compressed audio, mouse, and VGA or VGA+ display capable of displaying 256 colors or an equivalent PC with Multimedia upgrade kit. MS-DOS operating system version 3.1 or later. Microsoft Windows operating system 3.1. MS-DOS CD-ROM Extensions (MSCDEX) version 2.2 or later. Multimedia Strauss can also be played as an audio compact disc.

results.title: Multimedia Mozart: the dissonant quartet (PC)
results.brief_description: The Dissonant Quartet is an elucidating, captivating exploration of the man and his music, from the emotional dynamics of the String Quartet in C Major, to the historic events in his world, to the expressive beauty of the individual stringed instruments. Experience the power of Mozart’s artistry and the intensity of his genius. SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS: a multimedia PC or compatible with 386SX or higher microprocessor, 2MB of RAM, 30MB of hard-disk, CDROM -drive, audio board and VGA display. Microsoft Windows and MS-DOS operating system, versions 3.1 or later.
